verdicts.note: The record claims exists='yes' with url='https://github.com/mharnett/linkedin-ads-mcp'. This GitHub repo is NOT evidence of an official or dedicated MCP server from LinkedIn; it is a third-party implementation. The evidence provided (Microsoft Learn docs) contains no mention of any Model Context Protocol server. The correct value should be exists='no' (a third-party MCP exists, but no official/dedicated one from LinkedIn).
The url field should be dropped entirely when exists='no'. The GitHub link is to a third-party repo, not an official MCP server endpoint or documentation.

Evidence confirms OAuth 2.0 with r_ads/rw_ads scopes and Bearer token usage (getting-started doc mentions 'OAuth 2.0 and Permission Scopes' and 'Marketing APIs require the rw_ads scope'). The description of 3-legged OAuth, scopes, and Bearer token with Linkedin-Version header is consistent with the evidence.

Evidence clearly supports 'admin-approval': developers can self-register an app and get Development tier (up to 5 ad accounts) via the Developer Portal, but scaling to Standard tier requires LinkedIn's manual review/vetting and a support ticket. The marketing-tiers doc states: 'Click on View Ad Accounts setting for the Advertising API... LinkedIn reserves the right to review applications and select partners at its discretion.' The increasing-access doc confirms manual vetting is needed.

The notes accurately describe the tiered access model: Development tier (up to 5 ad accounts, unlimited read) is available after app review/vetting for rw_ads scope, and Standard tier requires a manual support-ticket application. The marketing-tiers evidence confirms these tiers and the increasing-access doc confirms manual vetting. No public paid pricing tier is mentioned in evidence.
Evidence confirms REST API ('ads-overview' mentions 'APIs provide all features' and references REST endpoints; 'getting-started' references https://api.linkedin.com/rest/adAccountUsers).

Evidence supports 'broad': ads-overview doc lists ad accounts, account users, campaign groups, campaigns, creatives, ad types, and analytics. The API covers comprehensive campaign management functionality.

The notes accurately describe the REST API surface (ad accounts, users, campaigns, creatives, targeting, analytics) and versioning via Linkedin-Version header. The ads-overview and getting-started docs confirm these capabilities.

buildable-with-caveats is correct: a developer can technically call the API once approved for Development tier, but full write access (rw_ads) and scaling beyond 5 accounts require LinkedIn's manual approval. This is a caveat, not a complete blocker.

The description accurately reflects the caveat: rw_ads scope and Standard tier access require LinkedIn's manual app review/vetting and support-ticket approval. This is confirmed in the marketing-tiers and increasing-access evidence.

The one-liner accurately summarizes the API: REST API with OAuth 2.0, manages Campaign Manager ad accounts/campaigns/creatives/analytics, but full access requires LinkedIn's manual developer vetting. All key points are supported by evidence.
'Marketing, Ads, Email and Social' is appropriate for LinkedIn Ads API focused on campaign management and advertising.
The website 'learn.microsoft.com/linkedin/marketing' is consistent with all evidence URLs which are from the Microsoft Learn LinkedIn Marketing documentation.
All five evidence URLs are valid and directly support the claims in the record. Each URL's 'supports' description is accurate based on the evidence content.
0.75 confidence is reasonable. The evidence strongly supports the technical details and access model, though some nuances about the approval process might warrant not going to 0.9+.

All three flags are accurate: admin-approval is required for write scope (rw_ads), access is tiered with manual review (Development vs Standard), and the MCP server referenced is third-party (not official).

revised.name: LinkedIn Ads
revised.one_liner: LinkedIn Ads (Marketing/Advertising API) lets developers manage Campaign Manager ad accounts, campaigns, creatives, and analytics via a REST API secured with OAuth 2.0, but full access requires LinkedIn's manual developer vetting process.

auth_methods.notes: 3-legged OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow; requires r_ads/rw_ads scopes granted via LinkedIn Developer Portal app; access token sent as Bearer token plus a Linkedin-Version header on each request.

revised.self_serve_notes: Developers can self-register an app in the LinkedIn Developer Portal and get 'Development tier' access (unlimited read, up to 5 ad accounts) without payment, but this is gated by LinkedIn's app review/vetting for rw_ads scope. Scaling beyond dev tier (Standard tier) requires a manual support-ticket application and approval — no fully self-serve, instant, unrestricted access. No paid public pricing tier exists; it's a free but access-controlled program.

api_surface.notes: Covers ad accounts, account users, campaign groups, campaigns, creatives, targeting, and analytics/reporting endpoints, versioned via Linkedin-Version header (e.g., https://api.linkedin.com/rest/adAccountUsers).

revised.main_blocker: Access to write scopes (rw_ads) and scaling beyond the 5-account Development tier requires LinkedIn's manual app review/vetting and a support-ticket approval for Standard tier, so full self-serve automation is gated by LinkedIn approval rather than purely technical means.

evidence.supports: OAuth 2.0 with rw_ads/r_ads scopes required; dev tier limited to 5 ad accounts
Access tiers (Development vs Standard) and approval process
Access token generation process via OAuth
Broad REST API surface covering ad accounts, campaigns, creatives, analytics
Manual vetting/support ticket needed to increase access beyond development tier
